The '''Black Knights''', also known as the '''Black Knights of Malrev IV''',<ref name="CSWE-Dark Underlord">{{CSWECite|I|169|Dark Underlord}}</ref> were an alliance of [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] cultists forged together by the [[Dark Underlord]] on [[Malrev IV]].

==History==
The organization was created roughly 250 years after the death of [[Darth Ruin/Legends|Darth Ruin]], and suffered the defeat of their leader at the hands of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Murrtaggh]]. The Underlord's [[Unidentified Zeltron Sith general|Zeltron general]] led the defense against a [[Battle of Malrev IV|simultaneous assault on the Black Knights Malrev IV fortress]], a diversion staged by [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] mercenaries to distract from the Jedi assassination attempt on their leader. The diversion was a success and the Black Knights' leader fell.

The group's fate in the aftermath of the battle is unknown, although the Black Knights later reformed and rose up again a number of times over the following centuries, and were combated by the newly promoted [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] during the [[Fluwhaka revolt]].<ref name="TJP">[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force (real-life book)|''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force'']]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
In the [[Adventures of the Starkiller, Episode I: The Star Wars|second draft]] of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']], the "Black Knights of the Sith" (the "[[Knights of Sith]]" in the [[The Star Wars rough draft|rough draft]]) are named as the destroyers of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Bendu of [[Light side of the Force/Legends|Ashla]]. They were a group of [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] taught the ways of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|Bogan]] by a fallen [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] named [[Seig Darklighter|Darklighter]]. In time, the Black Knights became the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor's]] personal guard. They were renamed the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lords of the Sith]] in the [[The Star Wars: From The Adventures of Luke Starkiller|third draft]].<ref name="TMoSW">''[[The Making of Star Wars: The Definitive Story Behind the Original Film]]''</ref>

In the book preface for ''[[Star Wars: Darth Vader: A 3-D Reconstruction Log]]'', [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] is referred to as the "[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire's]] black knight."<ref name="reconstruction">''[[Star Wars: Darth Vader: A 3-D Reconstruction Log]]''</ref> However, he has no affiliation to the specific group beyond a common Sith affiliation, having come into existence [[19 BBY/Legends|1731 years]] after them.<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>
